text: Managing deadlines is a key skill in both academic and professional settings. One important strategy is effective time management—breaking large projects into smaller tasks with specific due dates. Prioritization helps too, as you should focus on the most urgent or important tasks first. Scope management is also crucial: don’t promise more than you can deliver. Learning to say "no" or adjusting expectations when necessary can prevent last-minute stress. Finally, communication is essential. If you anticipate a delay, inform your team or supervisor early. This builds trust and allows others to adjust their plans.
